## Active Feature: Cover-Image-First Blog Header (005)

**Branch**: `005-cover-image-header`
**Spec**: `specs/005-cover-image-header/spec.md`
**Plan**: `specs/005-cover-image-header/plan.md`
**Tasks**: `specs/005-cover-image-header/tasks.md` (generated by `/speckit.tasks`)

### Scope at a glance

Replace the overlay-style publication hero on the blog home page with an image-first layout: cover image full-bleed at top, description as plain text directly below, no separate visible site-title text. Files touched: `ghost_theme/custom-theme/index.hbs` and `ghost_theme/custom-theme/assets/css/screen.css`. **Zero Next.js changes.**

### Key design contracts

- UI contract (Ghost template variable bindings → DOM shapes): `specs/005-cover-image-header/contracts/header-ui-contract.md`
- Implementation research (full-bleed pattern, CLS, LCP, mobile/desktop height): `specs/005-cover-image-header/research.md`
- Manual verification checklist: `specs/005-cover-image-header/quickstart.md`

### Clarifications (resolved 2026-04-27)

- **Layout width**: Full-bleed (edge-to-edge across the viewport).
- **Height cap**: ~50 vh on desktop (≥ 768 px), preserving aspect ratio. No cap on mobile.

### Important notes

- The cover image is the LCP element on the home page → use `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` + `decoding="async"`.
- Set `width`/`height` HTML attributes on the `<img>` matching the source image's intrinsic aspect ratio to prevent CLS.
- Keep the existing `.gm-page-header*` markup and CSS for the no-cover-image fallback shapes (C and D in the UI contract).
- Remove the legacy `.gm-cover-hero*` selectors and markup entirely (they have no other consumers — verified).
- Site title is preserved in `<title>`, social/SEO metadata, the cover image's `alt`, and the banner's `aria-label` — only the visible text element is removed.
- Per Constitution IV, run `npm run build` as a sanity check (the Next.js site is not touched but the build gate must remain green).
<!-- SPECKIT END -->
